name: Brainfuck
comment:
A brainfuck interpreter in Elm
@docs run

values.name: run
values.comment:
Run Brainfuck source code with specified input.
Returns an output string.

    -- Double input '1' == 49, 'b' == 98
    run ",[>++<-]>." "1" == "b"

Input is taken one by one. If end is reached, 0 would be assumed.

name: Brainfuck.Parser

unions.name: Command
unions.comment: Represent one of the eight commands of the language.

aliases.name: Prog
aliases.comment:
Parsed Brainfuck program consists of a list of `Command`s to execute
and a symetric `Dict` of jumps of matching square brackets.
aliases.type: { commands : List Command, loops : Dict Int Int }

values.name: parse
values.comment:
Parse string to `Prog`.

All unknown characters are ignored. Parser assumes correct brackets
pairing and will crash otherwise.

    parse ",[>+<-]>." ==
      { commands = [ Read, LoopStart, Next, … ]
      , loops = Dict.fromList [ ( 1, 6 ), ( 6, 1 ) ]
      }

name: Brainfuck.Tape

aliases.name: Tape
aliases.comment: An unbounded `Int`-indexed tape of numbers from 0 to 255.

values.name: decrement
values.comment:
Decrement value at current position by one. Value is floored at 0.

    get (decrement empty) == 255

values.name: fromList
values.comment: Convert a list into a tape.

values.name: get
values.comment:
Get the value at the current position. If the key is not found, return
0.

values.name: increment
values.comment:
Increment value at current position by one. Value is truncated overflow.

    tape = fromList [ 255 ]

    get (increment tape) == 0

values.name: set
values.comment: Set value at current position. Value is clamped within 0 and 255.
